nodes.body:
`npm run lint` runs `eslint src`, but this package doesnΓÇÖt include an `eslint.config.mjs` (core/github do). ESLint will fail with ΓÇ£No configuration foundΓÇ¥ when linting this workspace. Add an `eslint.config.mjs` to `packages/ado` (can mirror `packages/github/eslint.config.mjs` / `packages/core/eslint.config.mjs`) or adjust the lint script to point at a shared config.
```suggestion
    "lint": "eslint src --config ../core/eslint.config.mjs",
```
Fixed in ded75fa

`workspace` is imported but never used in this test file. With the repoΓÇÖs TypeScript ESLint recommended rules, this will fail linting; remove the unused import (or use it).
```suggestion
import { authentication, window } from 'vscode';
```
Fixed in 72bc1c2

This section references the `create-pr` and `copilot-pr-review` ΓÇ£skillsΓÇ¥ and a `superpowers:code-reviewer` agent, but thereΓÇÖs no corresponding documentation in this repo (search finds these names only here). Consider linking to where these are defined/installed for contributors, or rewriting this guidance in repo-local terms so `AGENTS.md` stays actionable in a fresh checkout.
```suggestion
### PR workflow ΓÇö three-phase process

When creating pull requests, follow this full multi-phase lifecycle. Do NOT hand-roll a simplified version. The process enforces:

- **Phase 1 (Local Loop):** Rebase your feature branch on `dev`, run the full test suite (`npm test` at the repo root, or the appropriate package-level tests), perform a thorough code review (either manually or using your preferred review tooling), fix findings, re-test, and repeat until tests pass AND the code review is clean.
- **Phase 2 (Create PR):** Push your branch and open a PR targeting `dev` (for example, using `gh pr create --base dev` or the GitHub web UI).
- **Phase 3 (Remote Loop):** Address PR feedback and CI results: run any automated PR review tools available in your environment (e.g., GitHub code review, Copilot PR review), fix comments (ideally one focused commit per comment), verify CI, and resolve merge conflicts. Any code change in this phase triggers a re-run of Phase 1.

Key rules:
- **Never skip or shortcut the process.** Every PR goes through all phases.
- **Any code change re-triggers the local loop** ΓÇö whether from human code review, automated feedback, CI fix, or conflict resolution.
- If your environment provides helper tools or agents for code review or PR creation, you may use them, but the expectations above still apply regardless of tooling.
```
Fixed in 1fa38cb

PR metadata says this ΓÇ£Closes #11ΓÇ¥, but the linked issue includes ΓÇ£Pipeline monitoringΓÇ¥ requirements that arenΓÇÖt implemented in this package (only work items + PR reviews are added here). Consider updating the issue/PR linkage (e.g., donΓÇÖt close #11 yet, or split pipeline monitoring into a follow-up issue) so tracking stays accurate.
Fixed in 13d0e48

When `getUserId()` fails, this function returns without firing `onDidDiscoverItems`. That leaves the last-discovered PR list in ProviderRegistry, so the UI can continue to show stale PR reviews even though refresh failed (e.g., after token expiry). Consider firing an empty list (or otherwise explicitly updating discovered items) before returning so the provider state matches the warning/error condition.
Fixed in 8f68b88. Now fires an empty list via \	his._onDidDiscoverItems.fire([])\ before returning when \getUserId()\ fails, so stale PR data is cleared.

`wi.fields['System.Description']?.replace(...).slice(0, 200)` will throw when `System.Description` is undefined because `.slice()` is invoked on the (possibly undefined) result of the optional chain. Use optional chaining for `slice` as well (or default to an empty string) so work items without descriptions donΓÇÖt cause the whole refresh to fail.
```suggestion
        description: wi.fields['System.Description']?.replace(/<[^>]*>/g, '')?.slice(0, 200),
```

`reviewerId` is interpolated into the query string without URL encoding. ItΓÇÖs usually a GUID, but encoding avoids subtle bugs if the value ever contains reserved characters (and is consistent with how `org`/`project` are handled). Use `encodeURIComponent(reviewerId)` when building this URL.
```suggestion
    const url = `https://dev.azure.com/${encodeURIComponent(this.org)}${projectPath}/_apis/git/pullrequests?searchCriteria.reviewerId=${encodeURIComponent(reviewerId)}&searchCriteria.status=active&api-version=7.1`;
```

This test manually resets the providerΓÇÖs private `_isRefreshing` flag before the second `refresh()`. Since `refresh()` already resets `_isRefreshing` in a `finally`, this extra mutation is unnecessary and makes the test brittle against internal refactors. Consider removing it (or asserting the flag has been reset instead of forcing it).
```suggestion
    // Clear mock calls before second refresh
```

PR description/issue text says items are grouped by project, but this provider sets `group` to `${projectName}/${repoName}` (project + repo). Either update the PR description/docs to match, or change grouping here to be project-only so it aligns with the stated behavior.

The `project/repo` grouping is intentional ΓÇö for PR reviews, knowing which repository a PR belongs to is essential since a project can have many repos. Updated PR description is already accurate on this.

The provider treats an empty `workcenterAdo.projects` list as ΓÇ£org-wideΓÇ¥ (querying all projects), but this setting description doesnΓÇÖt mention that behavior. Update the description to note that leaving it empty will fetch assigned work items / PRs across the entire organization.
```suggestion
          "description": "Azure DevOps projects to monitor. Leave empty to fetch assigned work items and PRs across the entire organization."
```
Fixed in 4bb6dec. Updated the setting description to note that leaving the list empty fetches across the entire organization.

`_cachedUserId` is reused across refreshes without being tied to the current VS Code auth session/account. If the user signs out/in or switches Microsoft accounts, the provider can keep using the old cached user id and fetch PRs for the wrong identity. Consider removing this cache, or keying/invalidating it based on the current session (e.g., store the session id/account id alongside the cached user id and refresh it when the session changes).
Fixed in 4bb6dec. Now tracks the session account ID alongside the cached user ID and invalidates when the account changes. Added test for cache invalidation on account switch.
Fixed in fedd2a4. Added \provider.dispose()\ before reassigning to prevent leaking listeners/timers.

`getUserId()` calls the ADO `connectiondata` endpoint without specifying an `api-version`, while the other ADO REST calls in this package all pin to `api-version=7.1`. Add an explicit api-version query parameter here as well to avoid unversioned behavior drifting over time and to keep requests consistent.
Fixed in daea445. Added `api-version=7.1` to the connectiondata endpoint URL.

The code supports org-wide PR discovery when `projects` is empty (it uses `['']` and builds an org-level pull requests URL), but thereΓÇÖs no test covering this branch (unlike the work item provider which tests org-level WIQL). Add a test that constructs `new AdoPrReviewProvider('org', [])` and asserts the expected request URL/behavior so the documented "leave empty for org-wide" setting canΓÇÖt regress.
Fixed in daea445. Added test `uses org-level PR URL when no projects are configured` that constructs a provider with empty projects and asserts the org-level PR request URL.

Fixed in daea445. Refactored extension.ts to wire `onDidChangeConfiguration` ΓÇö providers are now disposed and recreated when `workcenterAdo.organization`, `workcenterAdo.projects`, or `workcenterAdo.refreshIntervalSeconds` settings change.

This new extension package doesnΓÇÖt include a `.vscodeignore`. In this repo, the core and GitHub extensions ship with `.vscodeignore` to exclude `src/**`, `node_modules/**`, test files, configs, and sourcemaps from the VSIX; without it, the ADO VSIX will likely be bloated and may accidentally ship tests/configs. Add a `packages/ado/.vscodeignore` mirroring `packages/core/.vscodeignore` / `packages/github/.vscodeignore` (and ensure it excludes `src/**`, `src/test/**`, and `**/*.map`).
Fixed in 2fc00c9. Added `packages/ado/.vscodeignore` mirroring the core/github packages to exclude `src/**`, `node_modules/**`, test files, configs, and sourcemaps from the VSIX.

`getUserId()` handles non-OK and JSON parse failures, but a network-level `fetch()` rejection will throw and get caught only by the outer `refresh()` try/catch. In that case the provider wonΓÇÖt show the user-triggered warning message and also wonΓÇÖt clear/update discovered items (leaving stale PR review results). Wrap the `fetch()` in `getUserId()` in a try/catch (or handle errors in `fetchAndPublishPrs`) and return `undefined` on error so the existing warning + `fire([])` path runs consistently.

Fixed in e4e563b. Wrapped the `fetch()` call in `getUserId()` with a try/catch so network-level rejections now return `undefined`, allowing the existing warning + `fire([])` path in `fetchAndPublishPrs` to run consistently.

`startPeriodicRefresh()` assumes `intervalSeconds` is a valid finite number. If the setting is mis-typed in user settings (e.g. a string), `Math.max(intervalSeconds, 60)` can become `NaN`, and `setInterval(..., NaN)` effectively schedules a 0ms loop (high CPU + API spam). Coerce/validate with `Number(...)` + `Number.isFinite` (or fall back to default / disable) before clamping and scheduling.
Fixed. Added `Number()` coercion and `Number.isFinite()` guard so non-numeric values disable the timer rather than scheduling a 0ms loop.

`startPeriodicRefresh()` should validate `intervalSeconds` is a finite number before calling `Math.max`/`setInterval`. A non-numeric value from settings can yield `NaN`, which results in a near-0ms interval and can hammer the ADO APIs. Consider coercing via `Number(...)`, guarding with `Number.isFinite`, and falling back to a safe default or disabling the timer.
Fixed. Same `Number()` coercion and `Number.isFinite()` guard applied here.

When running org-wide (empty `project`), this log message will read `WIQL query failed for project ""`, which is confusing and makes it harder to diagnose failures. Consider using a label like `organization scope`/`${this.org}` when `project` is empty so the message stays actionable.

Acknowledged. The empty-string case only occurs for org-wide queries which is an intentional scope. The project name is still useful for project-scoped queries. Leaving as-is since the empty string in the log makes it clear no project filter was applied.

For org-wide discovery (`project === ''`), this error will log `failed to fetch PRs for project ""`, which is ambiguous. Consider substituting `${this.org}` or `organization scope` when `project` is empty so logs clearly indicate what request failed.
Same rationale as the work item provider thread - the empty string in the log is acceptable for org-wide queries.

The activation function parameter is named `_context` but itΓÇÖs actively used (`_context.subscriptions.push(...)`). Consider renaming it to `context` to avoid the underscore convention implying itΓÇÖs unused (and to keep naming consistent with other extensions in this repo).
Good catch but this is a minor style nit. The underscore convention is used consistently in this file and the usage is minimal (only `subscriptions.push`). Leaving as-is to avoid churn.

`ADO_AUTH_SCOPE` is duplicated here and in `adoWorkItemProvider.ts`. To avoid accidental drift (e.g., one provider updated and the other not), consider extracting this into a small shared module (e.g. `src/adoAuth.ts`) and importing it from both providers.
```suggestion
export const ADO_AUTH_SCOPE = '499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798/.default';
```

Agreed this could be extracted, but the constant is small and stable (Azure DevOps resource ID). Extracting to a shared module adds a file for a single string. Will revisit if more shared constants emerge.

`System.State` is requested in the work item details `fields=` list (and typed on `AdoWorkItem.fields`) but isnΓÇÖt used anywhere when mapping to `DiscoveredItem`. Dropping unused fields reduces payload size and avoids maintaining unused types; alternatively, start using `System.State` in the item title/description if itΓÇÖs intentionally fetched.
```suggestion
      const detailUrl = `https://dev.azure.com/${encodeURIComponent(this.org)}/_apis/wit/workitems?ids=${batchIds.join(',')}&fields=System.Title,System.Description,System.TeamProject,System.WorkItemType&$expand=links&api-version=7.1`;
```

Acknowledged ΓÇö `System.State` is intentionally kept on the type (`AdoWorkItem.fields`) and in the field list for potential future use (e.g. surfacing state in the item title/tooltip). The WIQL query already filters by state so the field is validated server-side; the marginal payload cost of one extra string field is negligible. Resolving as a nit.

`workcenterAdo.refreshIntervalSeconds` description says values below 60 are clamped, but the providers treat `intervalSeconds <= 0` as ΓÇ£disable periodic refreshΓÇ¥ (they return early). Either clamp `0` to 60 as the description implies, or update this setting description to explicitly document that `0`/negative disables periodic refresh (to avoid surprising users).
```suggestion
          "description": "How often to refresh Azure DevOps data (in seconds). Minimum 60 seconds for periodic refresh; positive values below 60 are clamped to 60. Set to 0 or a negative value to disable periodic refresh."
```
Fixed in a8c4a84. Updated the description to document that 0/negative disables periodic refresh.

This extension uses `console.*` logging, but the core + GitHub extensions initialize an Output Channel logger (via `@workcenter/shared` in `packages/github/src/extension.ts`) so users can actually see diagnostics without running the Extension Host debugger. Consider adopting the same pattern here (create an output channel like ΓÇ£WorkCenter ADOΓÇ¥, wire it to the shared logger, and replace `console.*` calls) to improve debuggability and keep extension packages consistent.
Already addressed in c16b02f ΓÇö the extension uses an output channel logger via `@workcenter/shared` (`initLogger(outputChannel, ...)` in extension.ts).

`src/logger.ts` imports `@workcenter/shared`, but this package.json doesnΓÇÖt declare it in `dependencies` (unlike `packages/github`). Add `"dependencies": { "@workcenter/shared": "*" }` (or the appropriate workspace version) so installs/builds of this package in isolation donΓÇÖt rely on hoisting from other workspaces.
Fixed in a8c4a84. Added `@workcenter/shared` to `dependencies`.
Fixed in a8c4a84. Now logging `result.reason` in the rejected branch.
Fixed in a8c4a84. Now logging `result.reason` in the rejected branch.

`getUserId()` calls the ADO `connectiondata` endpoint without an `api-version` query parameter, while the other ADO REST calls here pin to `api-version=7.1`. This makes behavior subject to unversioned API changes and is inconsistent with the rest of the provider; add an explicit `?api-version=7.1` (and update the related unit test expectation accordingly).
Good catch ΓÇö fixed in efd4f14. Added `?api-version=7.1` to the `connectiondata` URL and updated the test expectation.

The activation log always says "activated, registered 2 providers", but `configureProviders()` can early-return when no organization is configured (so 0 providers are registered). This makes logs misleading; consider logging based on whether registrations were actually created (or move this log into `configureProviders()` after successful registration).

In the `Promise.allSettled` rejected branch, the error is stringified into the log message. Since the shared logger supports variadic args and will serialize `Error` instances with stack traces, log the rejection reason as a separate argument instead of `String(...)` so diagnostics include the stack/structure.
```suggestion
          `Failed to fetch PR reviews from ${target}:`,
          (result as PromiseRejectedResult).reason,
```
